T1_fisher.claim: Fisher info = 4(2k+1)^2, constant in theta, grows with k
T1_fisher.implication: k=7 crash is NOT Fisher-information vanishing; it is a p_k->0/1 branch-boundary numerical event (guardable)

noise_sanity_check.test: 80 X-gates (ideal identity) through FakeMarrakesh noise model
noise_sanity_check.verdict: noise IS applied; FakeMarrakesh Heron-r2 single-qubit error is low

conclusions.headline: The Exp 18 k=7 div-zero crash is IMPLEMENTATION-specific (TranspilingNoisySampler V2 wrapper), NOT fundamental IQAE geometry.
conclusions.T1_finding: Fisher info per shot = 4(2k+1)^2 — constant in theta, grows with k. The k=7 div-zero is NOT a vanishing-information point; it is a p_k->0/1 branch-boundary numerical event (guardable).
conclusions.T2_finding: qiskit CORE IterativeAmplitudeEstimation + a minimal correct transpiling noisy sampler handles ALL P in {0.3,0.56,0.7,0.9} WITHOUT crash, reaching k up to 8 (P=0.3) and estimating outer-zone P=0.9 accurately (0.9007). No k=7 div-zero in core.
conclusions.implication_for_finding10: The crash-based half of the P-safety-zone (P in [0.2,0.8]) evidence is partly an ARTIFACT of the wrapper bug, not a hard physical boundary. Outer-zone P is usable with a correct transpiling sampler that guards branch boundaries.
conclusions.caveat: T2 used a 1-qubit Bernoulli construction; Ember Exp 17/18 used a 2-qubit |11> encoding with entangling (CZ) gates that accumulate more noise. The STRUCTURAL coverage failure (Exp 18b: P=0.9, N=40, 87.5% coverage, p=0.048) is a SEPARATE statistical claim not refuted here and may still hold.
conclusions.next_step: Re-run with a correct 2-qubit |11> EstimationProblem (proper Grover reflection about |11>) to confirm the wrapper-vs-core distinction under CZ-gate noise, and re-examine Exp 18b coverage with the clean transpiling sampler.
